methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $335M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Tokamak Energy, founded by David Kingham and Mikhail Gryaznevich, is a Series C nuclear energy company that has raised $335M+ to date. The broader nuclear energy sector includes 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers such as TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage and valuation-tier clustering to support reliable median or revenue-multiple methods.

The primary applicable method is stage-progression valuation. Using the $335M total raised as a baseline proxy and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement yields an implied value of $570M. This reflects normalized capital-efficiency expectations as the company progresses toward later funding rounds.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $484M (low) to $655M (median $570M), anchored to the single stage-progression datapoint. Key uncertainties include the absence of sufficient peer comparables at identical stage and valuation tier—a constraint inherent to the nascent nuclear technology sector. Additional resolution would require comparable transaction or public market data from peers at similar development milestones, or forward-looking metrics (time to revenue, capex requirements) not available in the current dataset. Investors should treat this range as preliminary pending access to more granular peer or operational benchmarks.
